original_image_tags: A scanned page of lined notebook paper. At the top is a faded, stamped or printed block of text that is difficult to read due to bleed-through from the reverse side. The main body of the page is blank except for handwritten notes at the bottom. There are faint, ghostly images and text visible throughout, which appear to be bleed-through from the other side of the page, including what looks like a circular diagram or sketch.

elements.description: Page containing lined paper with faint pencil sketches and handwritten annotations. Visible elements include: a faint stamp at the top with text 'DVL' and 'HEREIN IS INCLUDED IT INFORMATION COMBINED', a small circular stamp on the upper left, faint pencil sketches of a face and various lines/figures, handwritten text at the bottom reading 'Interview w/ WLADYSLAW KRASUSKI 11/7/59 100-26505', and a document ID 'NW 90290 DocId:34714592 Page 5' in the upper right corner.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the page containing lined paper with faint sketches, handwritten text at the bottom, and a stamped/printed block at the top, but Description B adds specific quoted text (e.g., 'Interview w/ WLADYSLAW KRASUSKI 11/7/59 100-26505', 'NW 90290 DocId:34714592 Page 5') that Description A does not mention or contradict.
